OPPORTUNITIES

Curriculum and Instruction - Elementary Education (TLPL)

Fieldwork Opportunities

TLPL251 (Community, Learners, and Classroom Climate) typically is taken during the sophomore year. This course, which is part of the gateway requirements for admission to the Elementary Education Teacher Education Program, provides an exploration of teaching in the public schools. This course includes a three hour per week ﬁeld component where students support instruction and student learning while assisting with teaching duties.

TLPL 479G (https://app.testudo.umd.edu/soc/search/? courseId=TLPL479G) ( Field Experiences in Education; Tutoring) Taken in the fall of the junior year, this course, which is part of the gateway requirements for admission to the Elementary Education Teacher Education Program, provides an exploration of teaching in the public schools. This course is a semester-long tutoring experience, and is the second in a sequence of ﬁve ﬁeld-based professional experiences for students who have been, or plan to be accepted into the Elementary Education Teacher Preparation Program. The tutoring experience is designed for students to develop positive dispositions and attitudes toward teaching and learning. It is an opportunity to learn how to build relationships with students and to develop effective pedagogical skills.
